---
title: "EEG & Signal Processing"
subtitle: "An Interactive Tutorial for Future Physicians"
---

## Welcome

You're training to become a doctor. At some point — maybe in neurology, maybe in the ICU, maybe in the OR — you will look at a screen showing electrical signals from a patient's brain, heart, or muscles.

This tutorial will teach you to **read those signals**. Not just glance at them — actually understand what you're seeing.

## How This Works

Each module is **interactive**. You'll drag sliders, change parameters, and watch signals respond in real time. There are short quiz questions along the way — these are **not graded**. They're just checkpoints to make sure the concepts are landing.

**Module 8 is graded.** That's where you'll get real EEG data and find out what's going on with the patient.

## Modules

| # | Module | What You'll Learn |
|---|--------|-------------------|
| 1 | [Signals](modules/01_signals.qmd) | What electrical signals from the body look like and how to describe them |
| 2 | [Sampling](modules/02_sampling.qmd) | How we capture continuous biological signals with a digital machine |
| 3 | [Filtering](modules/03_filtering.qmd) | How to clean up a noisy recording to see what matters |
| 4 | [FFT](modules/04_fft.qmd) | How to reveal the hidden frequencies in a signal |
| 5 | [EEG Basics](modules/05_eeg_basics.qmd) | What the EEG measures, where electrodes go, and what the bands mean |
| 5b | [Montages & Dipoles](modules/05b_montages.qmd) | How EEG signals are displayed, cortical dipoles, and phase reversal |
| 6 | [Artifacts](modules/06_artifacts.qmd) | Telling brain activity apart from eye blinks, muscle, and noise |
| 7 | [Spectrograms](modules/07_spectrograms.qmd) | Watching brain rhythms change over time |
| 8 | [**The Challenge**](modules/08_challenge.qmd) | A real EEG recording. A real clinical question. Your job to figure it out. |

## What You'll Need

- A web browser (you're using one right now — good start)
- For Module 8: Python or MATLAB, and a willingness to ask an AI for help writing code

Let's begin.

::: {.module-nav}
[Begin Module 1 →](modules/01_signals.qmd)
:::
